next_step.current_state: Identity bootstrap complete: root tree, root AGENTS, and context loaded. Must inspect 99_system per rule before processing inbox note.

next_step.current_state: Inbox note requests OCR of bills related to Juniper. This is a structured-record maintenance task routed through inbox processing, so relevant workflow docs are required before acting.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Locate Juniper canonical record and related bills
next_step.observation: Read 00_inbox/000_next-task.md; it requests 'OCR all bills related to Juniper'.

next_step.current_state: Confirmed this inbox task falls under structured-record maintenance. Need OCR/migration guidance and schema before touching finance files.
next_step.observation: Read inbox-processing-v2-update.md, which says to remove the inbox item only after fully completing structured-record maintenance and otherwise avoid partial edits.

next_step.current_state: OCR workflow says extract visible data into YAML frontmatter using schema. Need migration rules for adding frontmatter to existing notes.
next_step.observation: Read ocr-records.md; OCR in this workspace means adding YAML frontmatter from existing note text using the matching schema.

next_step.current_state: Need finance schema to know required frontmatter fields for bills. After that, find Juniper canonical record and relevant purchases.
next_step.observation: Read migrate-records-to-frontmatter.md; it requires preserving existing body text verbatim and stopping if required schema fields cannot be supported by the visible note.

next_step.current_state: Schema loaded. To find Juniper-related bills, I need Juniper's canonical entity record and then finance records using its identifiers, not display-name-only guessing.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: OCR eligible bills if schema supported
next_step.observation: Read finance-record-frontmatter.md; bill records require record_type, bill_id, alias, purchased_on, total_eur, counterparty, project, and lines, with optional related_entity.

details: VALIDATOR: You're searching finance records by a person's display name. Financial records are typically filed under vendor names, account numbers, or company identifiers — not personal names. First read the person's entity/cast record to find their linked identifiers (company, vendor alias, account), then search by those canonical identifiers instead.

next_step.current_state: Juniper canonical record confirms alias 'juniper' and kind 'system'. Next I need finance records linked by that canonical identifier.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Search finance records for Juniper identifier
Determine whether each can support required bill schema
next_step.observation: Read 10_entities/cast/juniper.md; Juniper is a system entity with alias 'juniper'.

next_step.current_state: Search using Juniper canonical alias found one matching purchase note. The validator warning is generic; I already used the entity's canonical alias from the cast record. Now I need to inspect the matched bill note itself.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Check whether required bill schema fields are visible
If complete, add frontmatter and then remove inbox note
next_step.observation: Searching 50_finance for the canonical identifier 'juniper' returned one matching purchase file: 50_finance/purchases/2026_01_16__eur_000105__bill__house_mesh_juniper_ssd.md.

next_step.current_state: The Juniper purchase note contains all required bill fields and line items needed for OCR into frontmatter. Before modifying finance files, I need the nested rulebooks for the finance lane and purchases folder.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Write frontmatter to the Juniper bill note
next_step.observation: Read 50_finance/purchases/2026_01_16__eur_000105__bill__house_mesh_juniper_ssd.md; it visibly contains the complete bill schema fields and two line items for Juniper.

next_step.current_state: Finance lane rules loaded. Need purchases subfolder rulebook before editing the purchase note.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Write OCR frontmatter for Juniper bill
next_step.observation: Read 50_finance/AGENTS.MD; finance records are sensitive, must be exact, and OCR work must preserve the original note body while using the system schema.

next_step.current_state: All required workflow, schema, and local finance rules are loaded. The Juniper bill note has complete visible data to support the bill schema, so I can OCR it by adding YAML frontmatter while preserving the body verbatim.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Write frontmatter onto the Juniper bill note
next_step.observation: Read 50_finance/purchases/AGENTS.MD; it permits reviewable purchase-record edits and only adds deletion guidance for purchase records, not for the inbox note.

function.content:
---
record_type: bill
bill_id: bill.house_mesh_juniper_ssd
alias: house_mesh_juniper_ssd
purchased_on: 2026-01-16
total_eur: 105
counterparty: Datenspeicher Köhler GmbH
project: House Mesh
related_entity: Juniper
lines:
  - item: 2 TB SATA SSD
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 89
    line_eur: 89
  - item: USB clone adapter
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 16
    line_eur: 16
---
# Juniper storage refresh

```text
+----------------+-----------------------------+
| field          | value                       |
+----------------+-----------------------------+
| record_type    | bill                        |
| bill_id        | bill.house_mesh_juniper_ssd |
| alias          | house_mesh_juniper_ssd      |
| purchased_on   | 2026-01-16                  |
| total_eur      | 105                         |
| counterparty   | Datenspeicher Köhler GmbH   |
| project        | House Mesh                  |
| related_entity | Juniper                     |
+----------------+-----------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item              | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | 2 TB SATA SSD     | 1   | 89       | 89       |
| 2 | USB clone adapter | 1   | 16       | 16       |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL             |     |          | 105      |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

Part boring maintenance, part trust-building because Juniper backups were becoming real household infrastructure.

next_step.current_state: OCR write succeeded for the Juniper bill. This was not an outbox write despite the reactive verification notice. Before deleting the processed inbox item, I should load the local inbox rulebook because I entered 00_inbox earlier without reading its nested AGENTS file.

next_step.current_state: Inbox rulebook confirms deleting the processed inbox file after full completion. The task is complete: the only Juniper-related bill found via the canonical alias was OCRed successfully.
next_step.observation: Read 00_inbox/AGENTS.MD; it instructs deleting the inbox file after fully completing one inbox request.

function.message: Processed the next inbox item: OCRed the Juniper-related bill by adding YAML frontmatter to 50_finance/purchases/2026_01_16__eur_000105__bill__house_mesh_juniper_ssd.md, then deleted the completed inbox note.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed root bootstrap rules, inspected 99_system before task work, used inbox-processing plus OCR/migration workflows, used the finance schema, preserved the original bill body verbatim beneath frontmatter, and deleted the inbox item only after full completion.
function.outcome_justification: The inbox request in 00_inbox/000_next-task.md asked to OCR all bills related to Juniper. The canonical entity record 10_entities/cast/juniper.md provided the identifier 'juniper'. Searching finance by that canonical identifier found one matching purchase record, 50_finance/purchases/2026_01_16__eur_000105__bill__house_mesh_juniper_ssd.md. That note visibly contained every required bill-schema field and line item defined in 99_system/schemas/finance-record-frontmatter.md, so adding YAML frontmatter completed the OCR task without guessing. Inbox rules then required deleting the processed inbox file.

enforcer_verdict: mutation integrity: agent claims operations that don't match the 2 actual mutation(s). Agent claimed to write frontmatter to an existing file but actually created a new file; the claimed write operation doesn't match the actual file creation with full markdown content.. Re-check which operations actually succeeded.
